name: Invoice
description: Payment requests: invoices, bills, receipts, and payment reminders. **Header** (`header`: identifiers, dates, **currency**), **totals** (net, tax, gross), and **`payments`** at document root. `statements[]` — line tables only. **`total_outstanding`** vs **`totals.invoice_total`** and **payments**.
system_prompt:
**Body-only tool** — Do not output `vendor` or `buyer` (the parties pipeline provides them). Fill **`header`** (`document_kind`, **currency**, `invoice_number`, `order_number`, `customer_number`, dates, `referenced_invoice_numbers`, **`reference_entries`** for extra labeled IDs), **`totals`**, **`payments`** (all paid amounts — no separate roll-up field), `total_outstanding`, `statements` (line content only), `payment_instructions`, and every other schema field.

**Root vs statements** — Put identifiers and **currency** under **`header`**. Put **`totals`** and **`payments`** on the root. Use **`statements[0].line_items`** (or **`line_groups`**) **only** for position tables.

**`header.reference_entries`** — For lines like **Abrechnungsnummer**, **Gläubiger-ID**, **Ihre Referenz**, **Einlieferungszeitraum**, **Genutztes Versandprodukt**, **Blatt-/ICR-Nummern**: one **`{kind,label,value}`** per row; **`kind`** = normalized key; **`label`** = printed heading; **`value`** = text/RFC as shown. Prefer **`creditor_identifier`** for *Gläubiger-ID*, **`billing_number`** for *Abrechnungsnummer*, **`carrier_product_service`** for product lines, **`your_reference`** for *Ihre Referenz*, **`sheet_batch_or_icr_range`** for numbered sheet ranges.

**MwSt table** — **`totals.tax_breakdown`**: one object per rate with **`tax_rate_percent`** + **`tax_amount`**. Set **`totals.tax_total`** when a single tax sum is printed.

**Service / billing periods** — Per **`statements[]`** block use **`service_period_normalized`** (**YYYY-MM-DD** range or month/quarter fields) when a **Leistungszeitraum** is shown; keep **`service_period`** verbatim. For **`line_groups`**, fill **`context_period_normalized** similarly. **Positions (`line_items` / `line_groups.rows`)** — **`title`** vs **`description`**: put the headline or first printed line in **`title`** when indented **a.**/**b.** (or bullets) continue below; put **only** those sub-lines in **`description`**. Never repeat **`title`** text inside **`description`**. Use newline-separated text in **`description`** for multi-line sub-blocks. Prefer **`description`** only (**`title`** null) when there are no indented continuations. Set **`quantity_unit`** (verbatim) and optional **`quantity_measure`**. **Vendor / SEPA** — Use **`vendor.banking_accounts`** for each creditor block (IBAN/BIC + **`creditor_identifier`** when shown); for Lastschrift/Bankeinzug use **`purpose`** `direct_debit_collection` and fill **`sepa_mandate_reference`** (*Mandat*…). Put the debtor account printed for the debit (*von Ihrem Konto*, IBAN/BIC, bank name) in **`buyer.banking_accounts`** with **`purpose`** `sepa_debit_debtor`. Prefer **`header.reference_entries`** rows (`creditor_identifier`, `sepa_mandate_reference`) for labeled Gläubiger-ID/Mandat lines. **`payment_instructions`** may stay as verbatim prose. Use **`org_public_record`** for imprint: **locations**, **identifiers**, **contact_channels**, **officer_groups**.

**Issue date** — **`header.issue_date`** is **Rechnungsdatum** when printed.

**Payments** — Use **`payments[]` only** for every amount received (itemized or one summary row).

**Totals reconciliation** — **`totals.invoice_total`** and **`total_outstanding`** vs sum of **`payments`**.

header.description: Document header row: invoice identifiers, billing dates, ISO currency, and kind. Keep these fields together; do not duplicate them under `statements`.

document_kind.description: Invoice sub-type. Required. Choose the single best fit.

letter_date.description: Date on the letter or cover. YYYY-MM-DD if unambiguous.

due_date.description: Payment due date if stated for the document.

referenced_invoice_numbers.description: Invoice or statement identifiers listed in the header — one string per number, never comma-merged.

issue_date.description: **Invoice / Rechnungs** issue date when printed (Rechnungsdatum, Ausstellungsdatum). YYYY-MM-DD. Not the service/billing period alone.

invoice_number.description: **Primary** invoice or Rechnungsnummer at **document root** (not under `statements`).

order_number.description: **Order / Auftrag / Bestell** reference when distinct from the invoice #. Root field.

customer_number.description: **Printed customer / debtor account** at the issuer (Kunden-Nr., Kundennummer). Use **`reference_entries`** for carrier-specific **`Abrechnungsnummer`** and other labeled IDs that are not your generic Kunden-Nr.; do not stuff composite billing IDs into **`customer_number`**.

reference_entries.description: Structured **labeled header references** beyond one-line scalars (**`invoice_number`**, **`order_number`**, …). One row per printed label/value (DRY schema, same shape for shipment, creditor, billing, batch, period). Omit empty rows.

kind.description: **Normalized category** for filtering and dashboards; use printed **`label`** for exact wording.

sub_kind.description: Optional finer discriminator (SEPA Creditor-ID vs IBAN creditor, tariff name, tariff code).

value.description: Primary identifier or free-text value as printed.

value_end.description: **Optional.** End of a range (e.g. second sheet **248**) or ISO **YYYY-MM-DD** end date when **`value`** is the start (`value` / **`value_end`** should not duplicate a single fused string).

identity_id.description: Resolved identity UUID from the party pipeline (for UI); not printed on the source document.

name.description: Legal entity name only (the contracting party), not a combined string with a contact person on the same line — use **contact** fields for a named representative.

contact_name.description: Ansprechpartner / contact person (natural person) when the document lists them next to the company; keep **name** to the company only.

contact_identity_id.description: UUID of a **person** identity when the contact is stored separately (from party step `vendor_representative`); links in the Identities view.

country.description: Full country name in English (e.g. Germany, United States) — not a two-letter code. If the document shows a code, expand to the name.

bank.description: Single **creditor bank** block; prefer **`banking_accounts`** when multiple IBANs or SEPA blocks appear — you may mirror the primary row here.

bank_name.description: Bank or branch name as printed.

banking_accounts.description: **All printed bank / SEPA payment blocks** for the issuer (e.g. *Kontoverbindung*, separate direct-debit account). Prefer over a lone **`bank`** when more than one IBAN or when **Gläubiger-ID** is tied to collections. Omit or `[]` if absent.

purpose.description: Why this block exists when distinguishable.

creditor_identifier.description: **SEPA Creditor identifier** (e.g. *Gläubiger-ID*) printed with or next to this account / mandate block.

sepa_mandate_reference.description: **SEPA mandate reference** (*Mandatsreferenz*, e.g. *Mandat* BOE…) when shown with Lastschrift / Bankeinzug for this creditor block.

org_public_record.description: Generic **imprint / legal disclosure** for the **vendor** (register seats, HRB, VAT, extra addresses, board lists, extra web/email/phone lines) — not the primary letterhead address (that stays on root **street** / **city** fields).

locations.description: Additional structured addresses (billing office, registered seat, branch, *Abrechnungsstelle*, *Hausadresse*).

role.description: Abstract role of this address block.

heading_printed.description: Verbatim printed heading above the address.

organization_name.description: Legal name line if it differs from the main **vendor.name** (e.g. parent *Deutsche Post AG* vs *DHL Paket GmbH*).

country.description: Full English country name when known.

identifiers.description: Registers, VAT, national tax numbers, chamber IDs — one object per printed line.

issuer.description: Register court, city, or authority (*Registergericht Bonn*).

contact_channels.description: Extra contact lines beyond the main **email** / **phone** (e.g. imprint *www.*, billing fax).

officer_groups.description: Management / board / supervisory lists when printed as named groups.

identity_id.description: Resolved identity UUID from the party pipeline (for UI); not printed on the source document.

name.description: Customer / bill-to **company or legal name** only — not a merged "Name, Company" string; the named individual belongs in **contact** fields and may be a separate `buyer_representative` identity.

contact_name.description: Ansprechpartner / contact or representative (person) for this bill-to, when shown separately on the document.

contact_identity_id.description: UUID of a **person** identity for the contact (from party step `buyer_representative`).

country.description: Full country name in English (e.g. Germany, United States) — not a two-letter code. If the document shows a code, expand to the name.

banking_accounts.description: **Printed debtor / buyer bank rows** especially for **SEPA direct debit** (*Lastschrift*, *von Ihrem Konto*): IBAN/BIC/**bank_name** of the account **to be debited** — not the creditor’s receipt account (that stays under **`vendor.banking_accounts`**). Omit `[]` if absent.

purpose.description: Why this block exists when distinguishable.

label_printed.description: Section heading as printed if any.

payment_instructions.description: Verbatim **payment / bank prose** when useful; **also** fill **`vendor.banking_accounts`**, **`buyer.banking_accounts`**, and **`header.reference_entries`** for *Gläubiger-ID* / *Mandat* so data is not only buried here.

totals.description: Consolidated document-level money roll-ups: net subtotal, VAT by rate, tax sum, and gross total.

subtotal.description: Net subtotal (Zwischensumme / net before tax) for the **whole document** when one line is printed.

tax_breakdown.description: When the document prints a **tax summary by rate** (e.g. *MwSt Gruppe* *A 19%* / *B 7%*, or columns *USt. %* + net + tax + gross), one object per **distinct** rate. Omit the array only if there is no per-rate table. Each row must give the **numeric** rate in `tax_rate_percent` (not only a letter).

tax_rate_percent.description: **Required.** VAT / MwSt rate as a number (0–100) exactly as in the tax summary (e.g. 19, 7, 0).

tax_group_letter.description: Optional letter in *MwSt Gruppe* (e.g. *A* on the same line as *19%*).

tax_rate_text.description: Optional verbatim label; `tax_rate_percent` remains the source of truth.

net_subtotal.description: Net / taxable base for this rate in document currency (e.g. Zwischensumme ohne USt.).

tax_amount.description: **Required** when the table shows a MwSt / VAT amount for this rate.

gross_subtotal.description: Gross for this rate if printed (optional; often derivable as net + tax).

tax_total.description: Total VAT / MwSt summed when a single roll-up is printed. **Whenever** the footer shows tax **by rate**, fill **`totals.tax_breakdown`** with one object per rate: **`tax_rate_percent`** (number 0–100) **and** **`tax_amount`** — never only a lump sum without per-rate rows when the table lists *MwSt 19%* + amount.

invoice_total.description: **Gross** document total (Brutto / Gesamtwert) before paid/outstanding split.

payments.description: **All payments** toward this invoice — card/EC lines, transfers, or a **single roll-up row** when only a total paid is printed (same shape: `amount`, optional `date` / `method` / `reference`). Do **not** emit a separate scalar for amount paid. **No payments:** `[]`.

total_outstanding.description: Document-level **amount still due** after payments. **Unpaid / open:** printed balance due, or **`totals.invoice_total`** if nothing paid yet. **Paid in full:** `0`. **Partial:** remaining balance vs **`totals.invoice_total`** minus the **sum of `payments[].amount`** when those figures appear.

statements.description: Each separately headed or separately totaled billing block. Assign line items only to the block they appear under in the markdown.

section_title.description: Section heading as printed above this block.

service_period.description: Verbatim service / billing period line as printed (any language/format). Prefer also filling **service_period_normalized** with ISO dates when inferable.

line_items.description: Flat list of itemized rows (use for simple tables). If you use `line_groups` for tiered/SaaS usage, set to `[]` to avoid duplicating the same lines. **`title`** and **`description`** must not duplicate the same headline — see field descriptions.

title.description: Main position headline when the source visually splits it from **indented sub-lines** (e.g. **a.**/**b.**, bullets under **1.**). Put **only** that headline here (product + period if it sits on the same main line). Use `null` if the row is one unbroken string — then put everything in **`description`**. **Must not** be repeated verbatim inside **`description`**.

description.description: Detail text **only**: indented sub-lines, fine print, refs — concatenate **multiline** blocks with **`\n`** (or **`; `** if the PDF wraps one conceptual block). When **`title`** is set, **do not** echo **`title`** or repeat the headline here; include sub-lines (**a.** *Abrufe …*, **b.** *Weiterleitung …*) entirely. When **`title`** is null, carry the **full** line text here.

line_kind.description: Row role when the source distinguishes it (e.g. discount lines). Omitted for normal lines.

quantity.description: **Anzahl / Menge** numeric quantity only (no unit string).

quantity_unit.description: **Einheit verbatim** from the document (ST, Std., kg, Palette, …) — preserve odd spellings.

quantity_measure.description: **English-standard normalized unit family** (SI / IEC where applicable; US customary/imperial gallon where Anglophone invoicing prints them — still store **quantity_unit** verbatim). Map from the ME column when clear; otherwise null or **`other_invoice_line_unit`**.

unit_price.description: **Einzelpreis** / unit price before line VAT; column *Einzelpreis* / unit when present; paired with **`amount`** (Zeilen-Gesamt / line total).

tax_rate_percent.description: VAT / MwSt rate as a **number** (e.g. 19, 7, 0) from the line or from the MwSt summary row that matches a letter on the line. Required whenever a rate is identifiable; null only if the line has no tax indication.

tax_group_letter.description: Single-letter **POS / MwSt group** (e.g. *A*, *B*) when printed on the line; always pair with `tax_rate_percent` from the receipt’s tax summary table (e.g. *A* → 19, *B* → 7 in DE).

tax_rate_text.description: Verbatim tax fragment when useful (*MwSt 19%*, *reduced*). **Does not** replace `tax_rate_percent`.

position.description: **Pos.** column / position label when printed (e.g. 10, 20, 40 rub-64b).

article_number.description: **Artikel-Nr.** / supplier article code (Artikelnummer) when in its own column.

line_groups.description: Grouped line items by **position / category** (SaaS usage, usage tables). Each group is one printed block: **title** and optional **context** (period) are separate; **rows** are the detail lines only.

title.description: Category / position name only (e.g. Compute Hours, Egress GB). Do not put the date range here.

context.description: Verbatim subline (period / caption) under the **title**. Prefer also **context_period_normalized** with ISO dates when inferable (same shape as **service_period_normalized**).

rows.description: Detail lines in this block (resources, discounts, subtotals as printed).

title.description: Primary cell label when the table splits headline vs detail (tier, SKU line). **`description`** must not repeat this text. `null` if one string only — put all of it in **`description`**.

description.description: Remainder only when **`title`** is set; **multiline** sub-rows allowed (**`\n`**). Never duplicate **`title`**; if **`title`** is null, full row text.

line_kind.description: discount / subtotal / detail as in the source table.

quantity.description: **Anzahl / Menge** numeric quantity only (no unit string).

quantity_unit.description: **Einheit verbatim** from the document (ST, Std., kg, Palette, …).

quantity_measure.description: **English-standard normalized unit family** (SI / IEC where applicable; US customary/imperial gallon where Anglophone invoicing prints them — still store **quantity_unit** verbatim). Map from the ME column when clear; otherwise null or **`other_invoice_line_unit`**.

unit_price.description: **Einzelpreis** / unit price before line VAT; column *Einzelpreis* / unit when present; paired with **`amount`** (Zeilen-Gesamt / line total).

tax_rate_percent.description: Per-row MwSt / VAT as a **number**; if only a group letter is printed, take the % from the receipt’s MwSt summary. minimum 0, maximum 100.

tax_group_letter.description: Single letter on the line (*A*/*B*) with `tax_rate_percent` from the matching *MwSt Gruppe* row.

tax_rate_text.description: Verbatim label if needed; does not replace `tax_rate_percent`.

position.description: **Pos.** column / position label when printed (e.g. 10, 20, 40 rub-64b).

article_number.description: **Artikel-Nr.** / supplier article code (Artikelnummer) when in its own column.

context_period_normalized.description: Normalized **billing / service period** for analytics and sorting. Use **ISO 8601** **start_date** / **end_date** (**YYYY-MM-DD**, inclusive) when the source gives explicit dates. For **month-only** captions (**10.2025**, **Oct 2025**, **MM/YYYY**), set **calendar_year** and **calendar_month** and, when unambiguous, **start_date**/**end_date** as the **first** and **last** calendar day of that month unless the invoice states narrower limits. For **quarterly** captions (**Q3 2025**), use **calendar_year**, **calendar_quarter**, and derived **start_date**/**end_date** when you can infer standard Gregorian quarters. Omit or null when the document only gives non-parseable prose — rely on the verbatim **context** / **service_period** strings then.

calendar_year.description: Gregorian **year** when the period is year- or quarter- or month-bounded.

calendar_month.description: **1–12** month when caption is month precision without explicit day span.

service_period_normalized.description: Normalized **billing / service period** for analytics and sorting. Use **ISO 8601** **start_date** / **end_date** (**YYYY-MM-DD**, inclusive) when the source gives explicit dates. For **month-only** captions (**10.2025**, **Oct 2025**, **MM/YYYY**), set **calendar_year** and **calendar_month** and, when unambiguous, **start_date**/**end_date** as the **first** and **last** calendar day of that month unless the invoice states narrower limits. For **quarterly** captions (**Q3 2025**), use **calendar_year**, **calendar_quarter**, and derived **start_date**/**end_date** when you can infer standard Gregorian quarters. Omit or null when the document only gives non-parseable prose — rely on the verbatim **service_period**/**context** strings then.

calendar_year.description: Gregorian **year** when the period is year- or quarter- or month-bounded.

calendar_month.description: **1–12** month when caption is month precision without explicit day span.
